briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to replace 1965 zoning bylaw with 2026 version
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on repealing Burnaby's current Zoning Bylaw (from 1965) and replacing it with a new Zoning Bylaw 2026. This proposed change would also introduce new Development Permit Area Guidelines and reassign some properties to different zoning districts. If approved, the new bylaw would update rules on what can be built and where, affecting property owners, developers, and businesses across the city.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to comment on how the new rules would affect your property or business.
briefs.source_body:
7.1
BURNABY ZONING BYLAW - PROPOSED REPEAL AND REPLACEMENT
Purpose: To seek Council approval to repeal Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965 and replace it with the proposed Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026, including new Development Permit Area Guidelines and zoning district reassignment.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Housing Authority Year 2 operating funding request
briefs.summary: Council will consider approving the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation's request for Year 2 operating funding. This is a proposal to provide money to help the Housing Authority run its programs and services for another year. The decision has not been made yet — Council will vote on whether to approve this funding at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.source_body:
7.2
BHA - ASSISTANCE REQUEST FOR YEAR 2 OPERATING FUNDING
Purpose: To seek Council approval of the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation’s assistance request for Year 2 Operating Funding.

briefs.title: Proposed: 2026 property tax rates for Burnaby businesses
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on the proposed 2026 property tax rates for municipal, regional, and Business Improvement Area (BIA) properties. If approved, this would set the tax rates that commercial and industrial property owners in Burnaby will pay next year. The decision will affect how much you owe in property taxes for 2026.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this will directly affect your 2026 property tax bill.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Professional Services
briefs.source_body:
7.3
2026 PROPERTY TAX RATES
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for 2026 municipal, regional and business improvement area (BIA) property tax rates.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to appoint an Approving Officer
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal to appoint an Approving Officer. An Approving Officer is a person who has the legal authority to approve plans for new subdivisions (dividing land into smaller lots) and road closures. This is a routine administrative appointment, but it affects anyone involved in land development or property transactions in Burnaby. The decision has not been made yet — Council will consider and vote on the appointment at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.action_required: No action required from you at this time. Watch for the council decision if you are involved in land development or property subdivision in Burnaby.
briefs.source_body:
7.4
APPOINTMENT OF APPROVING OFFICER
Purpose: To seek Council approval to appoint an Approving Officer.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on retaining wall height variance at 8012 11th Ave
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a Development Variance Permit (DVP) for a property at 8012 11th Avenue. A DVP is a special permission that lets a property owner break certain rules in the city's zoning bylaw. In this case, the owner wants to build retaining walls that are taller than what the current rules allow. If approved, this permit would let the owner build the walls as proposed. The decision has not been made yet — council will discuss and vote on it at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.action_required: If you own or manage property near 8012 11th Avenue, watch for the council meeting date and consider attending to voice any concerns about the proposed wall heights.
briefs.source_body:
7.5
DVP #26-01 - 8012 11TH AVENUE - RETAINING WALLS HEIGHT VARIANCES
Purpose: To seek Council approval for Development Variance Permit (DVP) #26-01.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to award insurance contracts for property and liability programs
briefs.summary: Council will consider approving the renewal of insurance contracts for the city's property and liability programs for 2026. This is a routine annual decision to ensure the city has proper insurance coverage for its buildings, equipment, and operations. If approved, the contracts will be awarded to insurance providers for the coming year. This does not directly change any rules for businesses, but it keeps the city's own insurance in place.
briefs.source_body:
7.6
2026 INSURANCE PROGRAMS - CONTRACT RENEWALS
Purpose: To request approval to award insurance contracts for the annual property and liability insurance programs.

briefs.title: Proposed: Council to approve Heritage Commission trip to 2026 BC conference
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal to allow members of Burnaby's Community Heritage Commission (CHC) to attend the 2026 Annual Heritage BC Conference in Squamish, BC, from May 4-6, 2026. The CHC advises Council on heritage matters, and this trip would help members learn about best practices and network with other heritage professionals. The decision is not yet final — Council will consider the request at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.source_body:
7.7
2026 HERITAGE BC CONFERENCE
Purpose: To seek Council approval for members of the Community Heritage Commission (CHC) to attend the 2026 Annual Heritage BC Conference in Squamish, BC from May 4-6, 2026.

briefs.title: Council to vote on contract award for networking software licenses
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award for the supply and delivery of networking software licenses. If approved, this would authorize the city to purchase the software needed to run its computer networks. This is a routine procurement decision that keeps city technology systems working.
briefs.source_body:
7.8
CA - NETWORKING SOFTWARE LICENSES
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the supply and delivery of networking software licenses.

briefs.title: Council to vote on contract for new civic facility furniture
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award for the supply, delivery, and installation of office and facility furniture for a new civic facility. If approved, this contract would cover all the furniture and equipment needed to fit out the new building. The decision is on the agenda for an upcoming council meeting and has not yet been finalized.
briefs.source_body:
7.9
CA - NEW CIVIC FACILITY FIT-OUT, FURNISHINGS AND EQUIPMENT
Purpose:
To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the supply, delivery and installation of office and facility furniture.

briefs.title: Council to vote on contract change for external audit services
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed change to the city's contract for external audit services. This is not a final decision yet. If approved, the change would modify the existing agreement with the company that audits the city's financial statements. The purpose is to ensure the city's finances are reviewed properly. The specific details of the contract change are on the council agenda for discussion and a vote.
briefs.source_body:
7.10
CC - EXTERNAL AUDIT SERVICES
Purpose:
To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) for External Audit Services.

briefs.title: Proposed: Contract change for geotechnical work at Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed contract change (a formal amendment to an existing agreement) for geotechnical consulting services at the Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex. Geotechnical consultants study soil and ground conditions to ensure buildings are safe and stable. If approved, this change would increase the budget for these services on the project. The decision has not been made yet — council will discuss and vote on it at the upcoming meeting.
briefs.action_required: No action needed from you — this is an internal city contract decision. Watch for the council vote result if you work on this project.
briefs.source_body:
7.11
CC - BURNABY LAKE RECREATION COMPLEX GEOTECHNICAL CONSULTING SERVICES
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) for geotechnical consultant services for the Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex project.

briefs.title: Council to vote on Spring 2026 community grant recipients
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on approving the recommended recipients for the Spring 2026 Community Grant program. This is a proposed decision — the grants have not yet been approved. If passed, the grants would provide funding to local community groups and non-profit organizations. The decision will be made at an upcoming council meeting.
briefs.source_body:
8.1
ECC - SPRING 2026 COMMUNITY GRANT APPLICATIONS
Purpose: To seek Council approval for the recommended grant recipients for the Spring 2026 intake.

briefs.title: Proposed: Mid-year 2026 fee changes to Burnaby business licences
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on mid-year 2026 amendments to the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw. This proposed bylaw would change certain fees for business licences, permits, and other city services. The changes are not yet approved — they are up for discussion and a vote at an upcoming council meeting. If passed, the new fees would take effect later in 2026.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if passed, your business licence or permit fees could change mid-year.
briefs.source_body:
8.2
FMC - BURNABY CONSOLIDATED FEES AND CHARGES BYLAW - 2026 MID-YEAR AMENDMENTS
Purpose: To propose mid-year 2026 fee amendments to certain fee schedules within the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw up for council vote
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed Tenant Protection Bylaw and framework. If approved, this new bylaw (a local law) would create new rules to protect renters in the city. The proposal includes a framework that outlines how the bylaw would work. Council will consider endorsing the plan at an upcoming meeting. This is not yet law — it is a proposal that council must vote on before it takes effect.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if passed, this bylaw could affect how you manage rental properties or your rights as a tenant.
briefs.source_body:
8.3
PDC - PROPOSED TENANT PROTECTION FRAMEWORK AND TENANT PROTECTION BYLAW AND ASSOCIATED BYLAW AMENDMENTS
Purpose: To seek Council endorsement of the proposed tenant protection framework and Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw and associated bylaw amendments.

briefs.title: Council to vote on Southeast Bikeway Upgrade Project improvements
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on proposed improvements for the Southeast Bikeway Upgrade Project. If approved, this project would add new bike lanes, crossings, and safety upgrades along the route. The decision is not final yet — Council will discuss and vote on the plan at an upcoming meeting. Business owners near the route should watch for the outcome, as construction could affect traffic and access.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, construction could affect traffic and access near your business.
briefs.source_body:
8.4
TC - SOUTHEAST BIKEWAY UPGRADE PROJECT - PROPOSED IMPROVEMENTS
Purpose: To seek Council approval of the proposed improvements as part of the Southeast Bikeway Upgrade Project.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to update design standards for safer intersections
briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposal to update the Burnaby Town Centre Design Standards. The change would require new developments to include 'protected intersection' designs. These are street corners redesigned to make it safer for people walking and biking to cross the road. The goal is to support safe travel for all ages and abilities. This is not yet approved — Council will vote on whether to move forward with the amendment.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council vote — if approved, new development projects in the Town Centre will need to follow updated street design rules.
briefs.source_body:
8.5
TC - PROPOSED AMENDMENT TO THE BURNABY TOWN CENTRE DESIGN STANDARDS TO INCORPORATE PROTECTED INTERSECTION GEOMETRY
Purpose: To seek Council endorsement to amend the Burnaby Town Centre Design Standards to incorporate protected intersection geometry, improve road safety for pedestrians and cyclists and support all-ages-and-abilities mobility.

briefs.title: Burnaby staff recommend against Brentwood Park SSMUH exemption
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will receive a staff report on April 28, 2026, recommending against exempting Brentwood Park properties from provincial Small-Scale Multi-Unit Housing (SSMUH) requirements. Staff state the City is bound by provincial law to zone for SSMUH, and restrictive covenants are private agreements the City cannot enforce as an exemption. A proposed Building Bylaw amendment to require covenant compliance would apply city-wide to all property types, adding application costs and processing delays, and is not recommended.
briefs.action_required: If you own a property with a restrictive covenant in Burnaby, monitor the April 28, 2026 council meeting for the final decision on this staff recommendation.

key_dates.label: Burnaby council receives staff report on SSMUH exemption — Brentwood Park

briefs.source_body: 9.1 BRENTWOOD PARK SSMUH MOTION - RESTRICTIVE COVENANTS AND STATUTORY BUILDING SCHEMES Purpose: To respond to Council’s September 9, 2025, motion regarding exempting Brentwood Park properties with Restrictive Covenants from Provincial SSMUH requirements. Brentwood Park SSMUH Motion - Restrictive Covenants and Statutory Building Schem

briefs.title: Council to receive building permit activity report for March 2026
briefs.summary: Council will receive a report on building permits issued in Burnaby from March 1 to March 31, 2026. This is an information-only item — no vote or decision is required. The report gives a snapshot of construction activity in the city, including the number and types of permits issued. It does not propose any new rules or changes.
briefs.source_body:
9.2
SU: BUILDING PERMIT ACTIVITY REPORT NO. 03 FROM MARCH 1 - MARCH 31, 2026
Purpose: To provide Council with information on construction activity as reflected by the building permits that have been issued for the subject period.

briefs.title: Proposed: Heritage designation for 3789 Royal Oak Avenue under review
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider an 'as-found report' and 'Statement of Significance' for the property at 3789 Royal Oak Avenue. A Statement of Significance is a document that explains why a building is historically important. This is the first step toward possibly adding the property to the city's Heritage Register. If approved, future renovations or demolition would need special permission. The decision has not been made yet — Council is reviewing the information now.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the next Council meeting agenda — this item may come up for a vote. No action needed now.
briefs.source_body:
9.3
CHC - AS-FOUND REPORT AND STATEMENT OF SIGNIFICANCE FOR HERITAGE RESOURCES AT 3789 ROYAL OAK AVENUE
Purpose: To provide Council an as-found report and Statement of Significance for heritage resources located at 7389 Royal Oak Avenue for information.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby solid waste and recycling 2025 annual report
briefs.summary: Council will consider the City of Burnaby's 2025 annual report on solid waste and recycling collection. This report summarizes how the city's garbage, recycling, and organics collection system performed over the past year. It is a proposed review of the system's effectiveness, not a final decision. Council may use this report to guide future changes to waste and recycling services.
briefs.industries: Retail, Food Service, Landlord & Property Mgmt
briefs.source_body:
9.4
EC - SOLID WASTE AND RECYCLING (SWR) 2025 ANNUAL REPORT
Purpose: To provide Council with a summary of the City of Burnaby solid waste and recycling collection system performance.

briefs.title: Council to receive update on proposed food sustainability measures in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to receive an operational update on proposed food sustainability measures. This update will outline current and planned actions the City is considering to improve local food systems, reduce food waste, and increase access to healthy food. No decisions have been made yet — this is a report to inform Council before any future votes on specific bylaws or programs.
briefs.industries: Food Service, Grocery & Food Retail, Agriculture
briefs.source_body:
9.5
EC - SU: ENHANCING FOOD SUSTAINABILITY IN BURNABY
Purpose: To provide Council with an operational update on food sustainability measures within the City of Burnaby.

briefs.title: Proposed: Safety improvements at Central Boulevard and Metropolis parkade intersection
briefs.summary: Council will consider proposed safety improvements at the intersection of Central Boulevard and the Metropolis at Metrotown underground parkade access. The proposal aims to make the intersection safer for drivers, pedestrians, and cyclists. A vote on whether to proceed with the changes is expected at an upcoming council meeting.
briefs.source_body:
9.6
TC - CENTRAL BOULEVARD AND PARKADE INERSECTION SAFETY IMPROVEMENTS
Purpose: To provide Council with an overview of the proposed safety improvements at the intersection of Central Boulevard and Metropolis at Metrotown underground parkade access.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on rezoning for hotel, more homes at 3900 Grand Promenade
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw amendment that would change the approved development plan for four properties near the Carrigan site. The developer wants to replace some planned office space with a hotel and restaurant, and add 362 more market strata (condo) units. This would make Tower 1B seven storeys taller and Tower 1A four storeys taller than previously approved. The developer also proposes to meet the city's inclusionary (affordable housing) requirement for Phase 1 only, and move that requirement to the Carrigan site instead of building it on these properties. This is a proposed change to a rezoning that was already approved in September 2024.
briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing or submit a written comment to Burnaby City Council before the vote.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Hotel & Accommodation, Landlord & Property Mgmt
briefs.source_body:
11.1.1
#14808 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 7, 2026 - REZ #24-23 (3900 Grand Promenade, 6950 Austin Rd., 9858 & 9898 Gatineau Place)
Purpose: to amend the previous rezoning of the subject lands pursuant to REZ #18-32 (Bylaw No. 14447), which received Final Adoption on September 23, 2024, and included two market strata residential buildings of 80 (Tower 1B) and 73 (Tower 1A) storeys, respectively, inclusive of a 13-storey retail and office podium. As part of the subject amendment rezoning application, the applicant is proposing to replace a portion of the commercial office use with hotel and restaurant uses, and add 362 additional market strata units from the development concept approved under REZ #18-32, resulting in an additional seven storeys for Tower 1B and four storeys for Tower 1A. In addition, as part of the subject amendment rezoning application, the applicant is proposing to provide the inclusionary requirement associated with Phase 1 only, to be transferred to the Carrigan site, which is an amendment from the previous rezoning approval.

briefs.title: Proposed: 12-storey mixed-use tower at 6535 Kingsway, Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw change that would rezone 6535 Kingsway. The plan is to allow a 12-storey building with 290 homes, shops on the ground floor facing Kingsway, and underground parking. This is a proposal only — it has not been approved yet. The project would follow the city's Official Community Plan (the long-term vision for growth) and the Edmonds Community Plan.
briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing or submit a written comment to have your say before Council votes.
briefs.source_body:
11.1.2
#14809 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 8, 2026 - REZ #25-09 (6535 Kingsway)
Purpose: to permit the construction of a 12-storey mixed-use multiple-family development with a double-height commercial storey at street level comprised of 290 residential units, ground-oriented commercial uses fronting Kingsway, and underground parking. The proposal is consistent with the applicable OCP and the Edmonds Community Plan designations for the site.

briefs.title: Proposed: Five-storey self-storage building at 2757 Norland Avenue
briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposed bylaw change to rezone 2757 Norland Avenue. If approved, the change would allow a five-storey enclosed self-storage building to be built on the property. The proposal follows the city's 1998 Official Community Plan and approved transition rules. This is not yet decided — the council vote is upcoming.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision date — if you own nearby property, consider attending the public hearing to voice support or concerns.
briefs.source_body:
11.1.3
#14810 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 9, 2026 - REZ #25-21 (2757 Norland Avenue)
Purpose: to permit the construction of a five-storey enclosed self-storage building. The proposal is being advanced in accordance with the applicable 1998 OCP policies and approved transitionary provisions.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby e-bike and e-scooter pilot bylaw amendment
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed amendment to the Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw that would create a pilot program for e-bikes and electric kick scooters. If approved, this would change the rules for where and how these devices can be used on city streets. The decision has not been made yet — this is an upcoming proposal on the council agenda.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this pilot could affect how your business operates if you use or sell e-bikes or e-scooters.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.1
#14811 - Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1961, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1964 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby e-bike and e-scooter pilot in parks
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would allow e-bikes and electric kick scooters in certain Burnaby parks as part of a pilot program. The amendment to the Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979 would create new rules for where and how these devices can be used. This is not yet law — it is up for approval at a future council meeting.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, this pilot could affect where you can ride e-bikes or e-scooters in Burnaby parks.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.2
#14812 - Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby bylaw amendment for e-bike and e-scooter pilot enforcement
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would update how the city enforces rules for e-bikes and electric kick scooters as part of a pilot program. The amendment to the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 would allow the city to issue tickets and fines for things like improper parking, riding on sidewalks, or other violations related to these devices. This is not yet law — it is up for approval at a future council meeting.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if passed, this could affect how e-bike and scooter riders are fined in Burnaby.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.3
#14813 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment Bylaw No. 3, 2026 (e-bike and electric kick scooter programs)
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby 2026 property tax rates bylaw
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would set the 2026 tax rates for all land and buildings (called 'improvements') in Burnaby. This bylaw is required under sections 197 and 216 of BC's Community Charter — those are the legal rules that tell municipalities how to set property taxes each year. If approved, this bylaw will determine how much property tax you pay in 2026 based on your property's assessed value.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this bylaw will directly affect your 2026 property tax bill.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.4
#14814 - Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to establish tax rates upon all taxable land and improvements pursuant to sections 197 and 216 of the Community Charter
